description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a prominent front porch supported by white columns and a stone foundation. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored siding, and the roof is primarily a dark gable style with multiple intersecting sections. The house features a wraparound porch, several windows arranged in both symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, and a main entryway accessed by a set of steps. There is a driveway leading to a garage, with two parked cars and a person visible, suggesting a suburban or urban neighborhood setting. The rear and side elevations show additional doors and windows, as well as a secondary porch area. The overall design blends traditional and colonial architectural elements, with practical outdoor spaces and clear access points.
description: A two-story light-sided house with a dark gable roof, wraparound porch, stone foundation, and a driveway with cars. Features include multiple windows, a main entry with steps, and traditional colonial styling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main porch with white columns, stone foundation, steps, and driveway with two cars and a person.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the intersecting rooflines, wraparound porch, and multiple windows on both stories.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the house, garage roof, and fewer windows, with the driveway and cars visible.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the secondary porch, rear entry steps, and a clear look at the driveway and both vehicles.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with steps, covered porch, white columns, and stone base
